Container.Name: firewall-live-log
Container.Overview:
A permanent, multi-device live firewall log dashboard. Each firewall or gateway ships its syslog to its own UDP port; the dashboard shows a real-time, colour-coded stream of every connection - source IP, destination IP, protocol, port, and the allow/block verdict - across the whole fleet at once, filterable by device, vendor, IP, and port.
Supports UniFi (UDM / UniFi OS) and Sophos Firewall (SFOS v18-v22) simultaneously, with automatic per-port vendor detection. Events are kept in SQLite with time-based retention, short-term history, and CSV export. Zero dependencies - pure Python standard library, one container.
SETUP: before the first start, copy devices.example.json from the GitHub repo into the data folder as devices.json and list your devices (one UDP port each). The container runs as UID 10001, so the data folder must be writable by it: chown -R 10001:10001 /mnt/user/appdata/firewall-live-log (or chmod -R 777). Make sure the Syslog Ports mapping below covers every port used in devices.json, then point each firewall's syslog at this host on its assigned port.
LOGIN: the dashboard requires a login. Set an Admin password below, or leave it blank to have a random one generated and printed to the container logs on first start (then change it at first login).
Container.Requires: A devices.json in the data folder (copy devices.example.json from the repo) and a data folder writable by UID 10001.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Dashboard HTTP port. If you change the host side only, leave HTTP_PORT alone; the container side stays 8080.

Config.Name: Syslog Ports (UDP)
Config.Description: One UDP port per device. Must cover every port listed in devices.json - widen the range (or add more port mappings) when you add devices.

Config.Name: Data Folder
Config.Description: Holds devices.json (your device/port config) and events.db (the SQLite event store). Must be writable by UID 10001.

Config.Name: Retention (days)
Config.Description: Events older than this are pruned automatically.

Config.Name: Max events (row cap)
Config.Description: Optional safety cap on total stored events. 0 = disabled (time-based retention only).

Config.Name: Devices config path
Config.Description: Path of devices.json inside the container. Leave at the default unless you move it.

Config.Name: Database path
Config.Description: Path of the SQLite database inside the container. Leave at the default.

Config.Name: HTTP bind address
Config.Description: Address the dashboard listens on inside the container. Leave at 0.0.0.0.

Config.Name: Prune interval (sec)
Config.Description: How often the retention sweep runs.

Config.Name: Queue max
Config.Description: Max in-flight events buffered before new packets are dropped (burst protection).

Config.Name: Admin password
Config.Description: Password for the initial admin account (created on first start only). Leave blank to have a random password generated and printed to the container logs, which you then change at first login.

Config.Name: Admin username
Config.Description: Username of the initial admin account (first start only).

Config.Name: Auth enabled
Config.Description: Require login. Set to false ONLY when the dashboard already sits behind an authenticating reverse proxy - otherwise it is fully open.

Config.Name: Auth database path
Config.Description: Path of the users/sessions SQLite database inside the container. Leave at the default.

Config.Name: Force Secure cookie
Config.Description: Force the Secure flag on the session cookie. Leave false; it is set automatically when accessed via HTTPS (X-Forwarded-Proto) through a reverse proxy.
